metrics.narrative: No quantified business case, pain, or ROI has been built over 132 days. The Salesforce fields Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c, and What_if_this_is_not_done__c are all null. The 20 Feb 2026 intro call covered a high-level S2P suite overview (iSource, iSave, iSupplier, iRequest, Merlin, iRisk, Merlin Intake) but the buyer (Stijn Immesoete, David Decoux) never articulated a specific operational or financial problem to solve, no cost-reduction target, no cycle-time improvement goal, no error-rate metric, and no savings expectation. The only signal is the 22 Jun 2026 notice that an RFP will run through Ariba, which implies a formal procurement process is underway, but the RFP scope, business problem, and success criteria remain unstated. Without a quantified value case, the Economic Buyer cannot justify a $200k 7-product S2P investment, and pricing approval is blocked. Quantifying the business case is the immediate next move.

champion.narrative: No champion has been identified or developed over 132 days. Stijn Immesoete (IT Product Manager Procurement) and David Decoux (ICT Sourcing and Category Management) are the only engaged contacts, tagged as evaluators in Salesforce contact roles. Neither has demonstrated champion behaviors: no introduction to the Economic Buyer (Isabelle Bellessort), no competitive intelligence shared (SAP Ariba is the named competitor but no intel on their position), no internal advocacy (no evidence they are pushing Zycus inside Etex), and no proactive engagement (127-day silence from 20 Feb to 22 Jun, then a procedural RFP notice, not a strategic partnership conversation). The 22 Jun reengagement by Stijn was transactional ('complete the Ariba registration'), not advocacy. Access to Isabelle is unmapped—Stijn and David have not brokered an intro despite being asked on 20 Feb to provide use cases and pain points for demo scoping, which they never delivered. If Stijn and David cannot or will not escalate to Isabelle, they are researchers without buying authority, and the deal is speculative. Building a champion (ideally Isabelle herself, or securing Stijn/David advocacy to broker the Isabelle intro) is the critical gate before any RFP response investment.

competition.narrative: SAP Ariba is the named competitor (Salesforce Competitors__c) and was confirmed on 22 Jun 2026 as the RFP platform host, which signals Ariba is either the incumbent or a strong contender controlling the procurement infrastructure. This is a significant competitive signal—hosting the RFP gives Ariba home-field advantage: familiarity, integration with Etex's existing workflow, and procedural entrenchment. However, no competitive intelligence has been gathered over 132 days: Ariba's incumbent status is unknown (are they the current S2P vendor?), buyer perception is unknown (satisfaction, gaps, replacement drivers), and pricing/terms are unknown. The fields How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, Why_not_Zycus__c, and Zycus_Differentiation_Why_Zycus__c are all null. The 20 Feb call did not discuss competition, and the 22 Jun RFP notice named Ariba only as the platform, not as a competitor being evaluated. The wedge to beat Ariba is Merlin AI (agentic negotiation, spend analytics, supplier intelligence) layered on top of core S2P, but this has not been activated because the buyer's AI appetite, Ariba's AI gaps, and differentiation criteria are unmapped. Running a competitive discovery session (ask Stijn/David: 'Is Ariba your current vendor? What gaps are you solving with this RFP? How important is AI/Merlin vs core procurement?') and activating the Merlin wedge in the RFP response are immediate next moves.
